sglang.title: [router] Forward all request headers from router to workers
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

To fix #3031
This PR is to enable passing api-key to the router.
Problem
We only copy content-type when creating the forward request to the worker. Therefore, api-key is not passed to the worker. To resolve the issue, we should copy all headers when creating the forward request.
Side Not

    ret = original_fn(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix k_proj's bias for whisper self attention
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Whisper has bias=False for k_proj in its self-attention, however, torch.empty will initialize the bias value randomly, and may cause overflow on key (this is very significant on CPU backend due to the device difference)
Use torch.zeros instead of torch.empty to initialize bias in ColumnParallelLinea

vllm.title: [Kernel] Flash Attention 3 Support
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Most of the changes for FA3 are in vllm-flash-attn, but there was some changes required on vLLM side. Namely FA3 doesn't support cu_seqlens_k when using a paged kv cache, instead it uses seqused_k which is the kv seqlens. FA2 also supports seqused_k so we switch to using this for both FA3 and FA2 wh

vllm.title: [core] add wake_up doc and some sanity check
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
wake_up does not appear in the api doc https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/offline_inference/llm.html . fix it by adding doc string.
also added some checks to make sure sleep and wake_up calls are in order. incorrect calls will be ignored with a warning.

vllm.title: [torch.compile] decouple compile sizes and cudagraph sizes
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
previously, compile sizes are limited to be a subset of cudagraph sizes. this PR decouples them to be independent.
use case: e.g. #9886 needs to compile for chunked-prefill size, but that is not in cudagraph capture size.
this PR also changes the user-facing config field to be compile_sizes and capt

vllm.title: [FP8][Kernel] Dynamic kv cache scaling factors computation
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR deprecates loading kv cache scales from json in favor of adding the option to dynamically compute them based on the first real input to the attention layer.
Our tests showed that the dynamic range computed based on the first input to each layer is representative of the entire model, and the

vllm.title: [TPU] Update TPU CI to use torchxla nightly on 20250122
vllm.author: lsy323
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Update TPU CI to use torchxla nightly on 20250122. It was on 20241017 which is very outdated.
To run the TPU CI script offline, in .buildkite/run-tpu-test.sh add export HF_TOKEN=$(cat ~/.cache/huggingface/token) for the HF token. And then run the test script to verify the updated nightly whl works.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix parameter names and `process_after_weight_loading` for W4A16 MoE Group Act Order
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Fix parameter names to ensure proper weight loading
Fix process_after_weight_loading if running group act order
Don't shard w2 weight_scales when running actorder for tp>1 and add is_k_full condition
Update parameter naming for clarity (intermediate_size --> intermediate_size_per_partition)

vllm.title: [Misc] Fix OpenAI API Compatibility Issues in Benchmark Script
vllm.author: jsato8094
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Modified the OpenAI API request payload to conditionally include the ignore_eos parameter only when it is set to True, as the OpenAI API does not support this parameter.
Updated the streaming response handling to safely append text even when the response element is None, ensuring robustness in respo

vllm.title: [Docs] Update spec decode + structured output in compat matrix
vllm.author: russellb

Update the feature compatibility matrix to reflect that speculative
decoding and structured output do not currently work together.
Related to issue #11484
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [V1][Frontend] Coalesce bunched `RequestOutput`s
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Under high load it's possible for the frontend per-request asyncio queues to back up, with the next token(s) arriving before existing ones are streamed back to the user.
In this case there's no reason for them to be emitted as separate outputs in subsequent iterations. By concatenating them into a s

vllm.title: Set weights_only=True when using torch.load()
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The default behavior of torch.load() uses pickle to deserialize
data, which is known to be insecure when used with untrusted data
sources. vLLM used torch.load() with data pulled from potentially
untrusted sources (huggingface). This could potentially allow
malicious code execution on a machine that

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Path join when building local path for S3 clone
vllm.author: omer-dayan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As arsed in issue #12311,
When running vLLM with S3 path, it clones the meta files locally.
Current state has a bug when building the destination path for every file:
When the S3 path ends with "/" it does not clone the files, due to bug in the building path algorithm.
The solution is to use os.path

vllm.title: Update compressed-tensors version
vllm.author: dsikka

vllm.title: [V1] Increase default batch size for H100/H200
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It seems the 2K token budget for API server is too small for H100 and H200. This PR increases this while keeping it as 2K for other GPUs.
Also, this PR increases the token budget for the LLM class from 8K to 16K, only for H100 and H200.

vllm.title: [perf] fix perf regression from #12253
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When I made the PR #12253 , I though self.decode_only = True is whether this model is decoder-only model, and therefore it is static.
However, it turns out this field means if the current batch is decode only batch (so that we can use cudagraph).
The bug makes every batch use previous batch's self.d
